hero.thesis: A newsletter delivering curated content, tools, and AI workflows specifically for solo lawyers can capture a portion of the 1.3M solo practitioners in the US, offering them value and efficiency in their practice.
hero.marketSize: 1.3 million solo practitioners in the US.
hero.payingEvidence: Lawyers consistently seek out professional development resources and tools, often spending $100+ monthly on industry-specific subscriptions.

ninetyDay.title: Define Your Unique Angle

actions.action: Research 5 top legal newsletters (e.g., "LegalBriefs", "Lawyer's Edge") identifying gaps in content.
Join 3 lawyer communities (e.g., "Solo Practice Hub" on Reddit, "Lawyers Connect" on LinkedIn).
actions.success: Engage with 10 posts to understand pain points.

actions.action: Draft a one-page newsletter mock-up focusing on identified gaps.
actions.success: Complete and receive feedback from 2 lawyers.

ninetyDay.metric: Defined newsletter angle and mock-up completion.

actions.action: Curate 5 articles/tools relevant to solo lawyers' efficiency.
Write 3 original content pieces focusing on AI workflows.
Design newsletter using beehiiv.
Create a landing page using Webflow to capture email sign-ups.
Set up ConvertKit for email automation and list management.
actions.success: ConvertKit account set up with landing page integration.

actions.action: Post about the newsletter in the lawyer communities joined.

ninetyDay.title: Launch and Gather Feedback

actions.action: Send the first newsletter edition to initial subscribers.
Collect feedback through a short survey link in the newsletter.
Analyze feedback for content improvements.

ninetyDay.metric: Feedback received from at least 10% of subscribers.

actions.action: Incorporate feedback into the next edition.
Experiment with 2 different subject lines for A/B testing.
Engage more in lawyer communities with content teasers.
Form a partnership with a legal tools provider like "BriefCase" for cross-promotion.
Launch a referral program with ConvertKit.
Host a small webinar on a trending legal topic.
Create a Gumroad account for managing newsletter payments.
Design a subscription tier model on Gumroad.
Run a limited-time discount for the first 50 subscribers.
Develop a mini-course on AI in legal practice.
Record first module of the course.
Promote course in newsletter and legal forums.
Introduce interactive content (polls/quizzes) in the newsletter.
Host a live Q&A session in Discord server "Legal Minds".
Analyze engagement metrics from ConvertKit.
Create a brand style guide using Lovable.
Update all content and communication to align with brand style.
Redesign landing page for consistent branding.

ninetyDay.title: Prepare for Scale

actions.action: Set up Notion for content planning and team collaboration.
Create a content calendar for the next 3 months.
Identify potential collaborators or writers for content expansion.

ninetyDay.metric: Content calendar and collaboration list ready.

actions.action: Launch a paid social media campaign targeting solo lawyers.
Offer an annual subscription discount to increase cash flow.
Evaluate growth metrics and adjust strategies accordingly.

toolStack.name: beehiiv
toolStack.why: Powerful newsletter platform with integrations for growth.

toolStack.name: Webflow
toolStack.why: Design and host professional landing pages.

toolStack.name: ConvertKit
toolStack.why: Email marketing platform for creators with automation features.

toolStack.name: Gumroad
toolStack.why: Simple tool to manage subscription payments.

toolStack.name: Notion
toolStack.why: Organize and collaborate on content planning.

firstTenCustomers.script:
Subject: [Lawyer's Name], Transform Your Solo Practice with [Newsletter Name]!

Hi [Lawyer's Name],

I'm [Your Name], and I've just launched a weekly newsletter designed specifically for solo practitioners like you. In each issue, you'll find curated insights, tools, and AI workflows to streamline your practice and stay ahead of the curve.

Join our growing community of solo lawyers who are already benefiting from tailored content.

Sign up here: [Landing Page URL]

Best,
[Your Name]
firstTenCustomers.cadence: 3 follow-ups over 2 weeks: initial email, reminder after 3 days, final call after 10 days.

pricing.raisingRules: Increase pricing by 20% after reaching 100 subscribers.

failureModes: Content Fatigue: Failing to keep content fresh and engaging.
Overcommitment: Spending more time than planned and burning out.
Underpricing: Charging too little, undervaluing your product.
Scalability Issues: Struggling with systems as subscribers grow.
Ignoring Feedback: Not adapting based on subscriber input.
Neglecting Marketing: Focusing too much on content creation and not enough on audience growth.

setup.llc: Use Stripe Atlas for LLC setup to ensure professional credibility and protect personal assets.
setup.payments: Set up a business account with Gumroad for handling transactions.
setup.contracts: Create a simple terms of service using LegalZoom for subscriber agreements.
setup.insurance: Consider a basic professional liability insurance policy through Hiscox to mitigate risks.

scaling: Once you've hit $3,400 MRR and are managing subscriber growth, consider bringing in additional writers or collaborating with legal experts for guest content. Use Notion to streamline editorial processes and manage contributors. Automate as much of the distribution and subscriber management as possible with ConvertKit to free up your time. If demand allows, explore creating premium content or workshops for additional revenue streams. Consider hiring a part-time community manager to foster engagement in your channels, ensuring your brand stays active and responsive. Finally, regularly reassess your pricing and value proposition to align with market expectations and your growing expertise.
